text: Writing clean code is an essential skill for any programmer. The most important aspect is readability—your code should be easy for others (and your future self) to understand. Use clear and descriptive naming conventions for variables, functions, and classes; for example, use `calculateTotalPrice` instead of `ctp`. Keep functions short and focused on a single task, which makes them easier to test and reuse. By following these practices, you improve the maintainability of your code, making it simpler to update and debug over time.
